From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [SP (disambiguation)](/w/index.php?title=SP_(disambiguation)&redirect=no "SP (disambiguation)"))

[![logo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/99/Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg/40px-Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg)

Look up ***[SP](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/SP "wiktionary:SP")*** or ***[sp](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/sp "wiktionary:sp")*** in Wiktionary, the free dictionary.

**Sp** or **SP** may refer to:

## Arts and entertainment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SP&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Arts and entertainment")]

* /sp/, the sports board on the Internet forum [4chan](/wiki/4chan "4chan")
* [*SP* (TV series)](/wiki/SP_(TV_series) "SP (TV series)"), a Japanese TV series
* [Game Boy Advance SP](/wiki/Game_Boy_Advance_SP "Game Boy Advance SP"), an upgraded version of the Game Boy Advance handheld video game system, released in 2003
* Standard Play, a magnetic tape speed used for [VHS](/wiki/VHS "VHS")
* [Story Pirates](/wiki/Story_Pirates "Story Pirates"), a podcast
* [Subdominant parallel](/wiki/Subdominant_parallel "Subdominant parallel"), a type of musical chord
* SP, producer stage name of [Mumzy Stranger](/wiki/Mumzy_Stranger "Mumzy Stranger") (born 1984), British rapper
* SP, stage name of [Krisztián Éder](/wiki/Kriszti%C3%A1n_%C3%89der "Krisztián Éder") (born 1988), Hungarian rapper
* *[South Park](/wiki/South_Park "South Park")*, an animated American television series
* [The Smashing Pumpkins](/wiki/The_Smashing_Pumpkins "The Smashing Pumpkins"), an American alternative rock band
* The SP line of [digital samplers](/wiki/Sampler_(musical_instrument) "Sampler (musical instrument)") marketed by [Roland Corporation](/wiki/Roland_Corporation "Roland Corporation") under its own name and the [Boss](/wiki/Boss_Corporation "Boss Corporation") brand, most notably the [Boss SP-303](/wiki/Boss_SP-303 "Boss SP-303") (2001) and [Roland SP-404](/wiki/Roland_SP-404 "Roland SP-404") (2005)

## Businesses and organizations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SP&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Businesses and organizations")]

* [*SP* (magazine)](/wiki/SP_(magazine) "SP (magazine)"), illustrated news magazine in Spain between 1957 and 1972
* [SP Technical Research Institute of Sweden](/wiki/SP_Technical_Research_Institute_of_Sweden "SP Technical Research Institute of Sweden")
* [ScottishPower](/wiki/ScottishPower "ScottishPower"), Scottish energy company
* Szkoła Podstawowa ("[primary school](/wiki/Primary_school "Primary school")" in polish)
* [Service provider](/wiki/Service_provider "Service provider"), company that provides organizations with consulting, legal, real estate, education, communications, storage, processing, and many other services
* [Silicon Power](/wiki/Silicon_Power "Silicon Power"), Taiwan-based manufacturer of flash memory products and other industrial grade computer products
* [Singapore Polytechnic](/wiki/Singapore_Polytechnic "Singapore Polytechnic"), polytechnic in Dover, Singapore
* [Státní podnik](/wiki/St%C3%A1tn%C3%AD_podnik "Státní podnik") ("state business"), former Czechoslovak entity type
* [Story point](/wiki/Story_point "Story point"), term used in [agile software development](/wiki/Agile_software_development "Agile software development") methodologies
* *[Suara Pembaruan](/wiki/Suara_Pembaruan "Suara Pembaruan")*, former Indonesian newspaper

### Government and military

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SP&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Government and military")]

* [Superintendent of police (India)](/wiki/Superintendent_of_police_(India) "Superintendent of police (India)"), a senior rank in the Indian police and law enforcement agencies
* A US Navy hull registration number prefix: [Section patrol (SP)](/wiki/Section_patrol_craft#Section_patrol_(SP) "Section patrol craft")
* [Security Police (Japan)](/wiki/Security_Police_(Japan) "Security Police (Japan)"), for VIP protection
* [Shore patrol](/wiki/Shore_patrol "Shore patrol"), UK and US naval security personnel

### Political parties

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SP&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Political parties")]

* [Centre Party (Norway)](/wiki/Centre_Party_(Norway) "Centre Party (Norway)") (*Senterpartiet*)
* [Socialist Party](/wiki/Socialist_Party "Socialist Party") or Social Democratic Party in many places, including:
  + [Socialist Party of Albania](/wiki/Socialist_Party_of_Albania "Socialist Party of Albania")
  + [Samajwadi Party](/wiki/Samajwadi_Party "Samajwadi Party"), in India
  + [Socialist Party (Netherlands)](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(Netherlands) "Socialist Party (Netherlands)") (*Socialistische Partij*)
  + [Socialistische Partij Anders](/wiki/Socialistische_Partij_Anders "Socialistische Partij Anders") (*Socialistische Partij*), a historical name for the sp.a in Belgium
  + [Socialist Party (Netherlands, interbellum)](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(Netherlands,_interbellum) "Socialist Party (Netherlands, interbellum)") (*Socialistische Partij*) 1918–1929
  + [Social Democratic Party of Switzerland](/wiki/Social_Democratic_Party_of_Switzerland "Social Democratic Party of Switzerland")
* [Swatantra Party](/wiki/Swatantra_Party "Swatantra Party"), an Indian classical liberal political party 1959–1974

### Religious organizations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SP&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Religious organizations")]

* [Piarists](/wiki/Piarists "Piarists") or *Ordo Clericorum Regularium pauperum Matris Dei Scholarum Piarum*, a Catholic order
* [Südtiroler Pfadfinderschaft](/wiki/S%C3%BCdtiroler_Pfadfinderschaft "Südtiroler Pfadfinderschaft"), a Catholic scouting association in Italy

## Places

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SP&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Places")]

* [SP postcode area](/wiki/SP_postcode_area "SP postcode area"), UK, the Salisbury postcode area
* [São Paulo (state)](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo_(state) "São Paulo (state)"), a state in Brazil
  + [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo"), capital of the state and the most populous city of Brazil

## Science and technology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SP&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Science and technology")]

* sp orbitals, in physics, an instance of atomic [orbital hybridisation](/wiki/Orbital_hybridisation "Orbital hybridisation")
* [Self-propelled (disambiguation)](/wiki/Self-propelled_(disambiguation) "Self-propelled (disambiguation)")
* [Soft-point bullet](/wiki/Soft-point_bullet "Soft-point bullet"), a type of ammunition
* [Spontaneous potential](/wiki/Spontaneous_potential "Spontaneous potential") differences in the Earth
* Stated Preference, a [choice modelling](/wiki/Choice_modelling#Stated_preference "Choice modelling") method
* [Surface plasmon](/wiki/Surface_plasmon "Surface plasmon"), a type of electron oscillation along metallic surfaces
* A [Unified Soil Classification System](/wiki/Unified_Soil_Classification_System "Unified Soil Classification System") symbol for *sand, poorly graded*

### Biology and medicine

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SP&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Biology and medicine")]

* [Sp. (biology)](/wiki/Sp._(biology) "Sp. (biology)"), abbreviation for species
* [Simulated patient](/wiki/Simulated_patient "Simulated patient"), for medical training
* [Substance P](/wiki/Substance_P "Substance P"), a neuropeptide
* Status post, notes about [sequelae](/wiki/Sequela "Sequela") with reference to their cause

### Computing and telecommunications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SP&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Computing and telecommunications")]

* [IBM RS/6000 SP](/wiki/IBM_RS/6000_SP "IBM RS/6000 SP"), supercomputer series
* [Service pack](/wiki/Service_pack "Service pack"), software update
* [Stack pointer](/wiki/Stack_pointer "Stack pointer")
* [Stored procedure](/wiki/Stored_procedure "Stored procedure")
* [Synthetic programming](/wiki/Synthetic_programming "Synthetic programming")

### Mathematics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SP&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Mathematics")]

* [Trace (linear algebra)](/wiki/Trace_(linear_algebra) "Trace (linear algebra)"), or "Spur" (German), of a square matrix
* *Sp*(*n*) and *Sp*(2*n*,*F*), a [symplectic group](/wiki/Symplectic_group "Symplectic group") in mathematics

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SP&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Transportation")]

* [SATA Air Acores](/wiki/SATA_Air_Acores "SATA Air Acores") (IATA code SP)
* [Saidapet railway station](/wiki/Saidapet_railway_station "Saidapet railway station") (station code SP)
* [Southern Pacific Railroad](/wiki/Southern_Pacific_Railroad "Southern Pacific Railroad") (reporting mark SP)

## Other uses

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SP&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Other uses")]

* [25 metre pistol](/wiki/25_metre_pistol "25 metre pistol"), a shooting sport formerly known as *sport pistol*
* [Aircraft registration prefix](/wiki/List_of_aircraft_registration_prefixes "List of aircraft registration prefixes") for Poland
* s.p., *[sine prole](/wiki/Sine_prole "Sine prole")* (Latin: "without issue" (i.e., having no children), full form d.s.p. *decessit sine prole*, "died without issue")
* Ś.P. [*Świętej pamięci*](https://pl.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C5%9Awi%C4%99tej_pami%C4%99ci "pl:Świętej pamięci") meaning holy memory is written on many Polish gravestones
* [Provincial road (Italy)](/wiki/Provincial_road_(Italy) "Provincial road (Italy)") (*Strada Provinciale*), a category of provincial [roads in Italy](/wiki/Roads_in_Italy "Roads in Italy")
* [September](/wiki/September "September")
* [Seri Pahlawan Gagah Perkasa](/wiki/Seri_Pahlawan_Gagah_Perkasa "Seri Pahlawan Gagah Perkasa") (S.P.), a Malaysian military decoration
* [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"), particularly used on maps to denote Spanish possessions, territories, etc.
* [Spanish language](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language")
* [Special (disambiguation)](/wiki/Special_(disambiguation) "Special (disambiguation)")
* [Spelling](/wiki/Spelling "Spelling"), especially to note a spelling error
* Spurius, the Roman praenomen; see [Spurius (disambiguation)](/wiki/Spurius_(disambiguation) "Spurius (disambiguation)")
* [Starting pitcher](/wiki/Starting_pitcher "Starting pitcher"), a team's first pitcher in a game of baseball or softball
* [Suppressive person](/wiki/Suppressive_person "Suppressive person"), in Scientology
* [Starting price](/wiki/Starting_price "Starting price"), odds applicable on a particular entry in a horse race (or other race) as the race is about to start
* [Superintendent (police)](/wiki/Superintendent_(police) "Superintendent (police)"), a rank in British police services and in most English-speaking Commonwealth nations
* SP, an abbreviation for the [Syrian pound](/wiki/Syrian_pound "Syrian pound")
* ["Shot put" athletics abbreviation](/wiki/Athletics_abbreviations#Events "Athletics abbreviations") in track and field
* [Nikon SP](/wiki/Nikon_SP "Nikon SP"), a 1957 35 mm film camera
